reviews.review_msg: Amazing prof!
she is really awful professor. It is better she passes some courses to learn how she should treat with student.
This prof should not be teaching period!! I won't be surprised if her teaching score is inflated due to the way the evaluation forms were handled!!

She is a grad student. Unable to communicate the material or does not know it well, so learning suffers. Very incomprehensible with a heavy accent.  I should have taken the STAT equivalent instead or wait for someone else to teach the course. Labs were unclear and too easy. Disappointing teaching and not of a university level.

This is the first time I took a course where the prof is also the TA. This is like conflict of interest. If the prof is useless then you end up being messed up at labs too. That's what happened in this course. Liliana appeared friendly but the materials was too hard for her to teach.

All I remembered from this course was knee-high black leather riding booths and tight fitting jeans. What was this course about again? I didn't learn much but got a good grade as they were handed out freely!!
Oh gosh!! Her teaching was atrocious and all over the place. Turned me off completely from stats. What a waste of my time and money.
The best formula to describe my experience is: boring teacher + boring materials + poor language skills + bad notes = horrible course!!

VERY HEAVY ACCENT. He is very hard to understand. He did not know the material and asked students to get up and teach the lecture. However if you have to take this class with Jax focus on the doing well in the labs they are 45% of your grade! This class would not have been nearly as frustrating if the prof knew how to teach statistics.

Lectures are a necessity as lecture slides posted online are not that helpful unless you have additional notes and context with them. Don't buy textbook. Go to the first few labs to understand how to do the assignments.

Shiv has great assignment descriptions and course organization on the canvas page. You have to attend lectures because the posted notes do not include everything. He poses questions in class that do not make sense and he can make students feel uncomfortable making them prove to him that their answer is correct.

unclear instructions for assignments and grading criteria
disrespectful to some students

The overall course was easy, there were only a couple of assignments. The tests were difficult, so study the key concepts and not too much on the calculations. If you do well on the assignments and not too well on the tests, you should be able to get within the B to A range.
GEOG 253. Class was unorganised but compensated by being very easy, would take for a GPA booster, but not to the most engaging learning experience.

Posts lectures late, does not explain content and labs super well. However, she grades fairly and easily and that is all that matters. This class is a GPA booster and I would take a class again with this teacher.

This prof would rarely post lectures and labs on time & took forever to answer emails. She always used lame excuses, such as not having wifi (Go to campus, or to starbucks!), being busy with her job, getting covid... This is the worst quality education i've had at SFU and don't recommend! It's not worth your tuition $.
Doesn't respond to any emails. Doesn't upload all the lecture content she says she's going to.

Peter was an excellent professor to have, considering he used to be a dean a UVIC and now teaches this course. Peter would have very engaging lectures, you can any questions you want to the TA or to him and receive comprehensive feedback or input. I don't even like math but I would take this class again.
If you love professors telling dad jokes, take a class with Peter Keller. This guy is a gem with dad jokes, lectures are so fun to listen too when he tells his amazing dad jokes all the time!!!

He makes extremely difficult exams, but if you study you will get a B becuase labs are worth so much of your grade the exams won't affect your grade too much. But he gets a 5/5 because Peter Keller has the absolute best dad jokes in the world. He tells multiple each lecture and it's so amazing. I laugh everytime at his amazing dad jokes!
Really enjoy taking this class!

He is ok but his slide have many small error that sometime I need to wonder whether he types wrong again or it is me who don't understand. Weekly assignment relates to slide and take time to work. Midterm is MC and ok, average is 68. Final is MC, few shorts and 1 long Q. It is pretty decent and you can choose 1/2 or 2/5 option for short Q.

Horrible professor. Gives you zero practice problems, only lecture slides absolutely riddled with spelling errors that you can't understand them, and you are expected to know how to solve complex equations on the exam. Dr. Keller needs to learn how to organize a course! There are no tools for you to study, you have to google stuff to learn...

I don't know if I've ever met someone as good with dad jokes as Dr. Keller!!! His jokes in class are always the best and make classes enjoyable. He also finishes covering material and is decent at explaining. However his lecture slides and lab assignments are filled with gruesome spelling mistakes, making it hard to understand what he is asking.

Great professor, but I wish there were more components to the course, like practice exams/questions or homework so that you can boost your marks. There is only the lab component (40%), midterm and final exam. There were mistakes in the lectures which often made the labs more difficult. Aside from that I really enjoyed this course and his dad jokes.
Most hilarious professor at SFU. The way he talks gives everyone chuckles and he is really good at dad jokes.
